entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume crop whose improved varieties were rapidly adopted by farmers.

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the study on the rapid adoption of improved chickpea varieties was conducted.

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: The broader region mentioned in the context of limited adoption of new technologies among smallholder farmers.

entities.name: ADOPTION
entities.description: The process of farmers integrating new agricultural technologies and improved crop varieties into their practices.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of improved chickpea varieties to resist diseases, which increased their attraction to farmers.

entities.name: SUPERIOR RETURNS
entities.description: The higher economic gains and profits associated with the use of improved chickpea varieties.

entities.name: MARKET ACCESS
entities.description: The ease with which rural households can reach markets to sell their produce, facilitating adoption.

entities.name: EXTENSION SERVICES
entities.description: The accessibility of agricultural advisory and support services that advanced the adoption process.

entities.name: RURAL HOUSEHOLDS
entities.description: The smallholder farming units in rural districts who adopted the improved chickpea varieties.
